notes: This image is a single-shot breakfast scene with one center bowl and three side items; no spatial/clocking or text-reading complexity. The discriminator between models is the completeness and accuracy of the congee-topping inventory (egg, scallions, ginger, fried shallots, cilantro, soy sauce), the drink ID (soy milk vs. milk tea/coffee), and the chopstick placement across the red plate.

Gemma4 26b and 31b are clearly the strongest: both identify all six toppings, the soy milk, the you tiao, the chopstick placement, the marble table, tiled walls and window. 26b is the most complete (adds cilantro that 31b omits); 31b is the most precise on chopstick placement.

The Qwen3.5/3.6 family is consistently strong on toppings but almost uniformly defaults to 'milk tea' or 'teh' for the drink — a systematic cultural prior. The Qwen3.5-9b variants hedge to include soy milk, which is why they score higher.

The Qwen3.6-35b-a3b|think entry is the only model that makes a clear factual error about the dish, calling the egg a 'raw egg yolk' rather than sunny-side-up; this is the main differentiator that lowers its score below its siblings.
qwen3-vl-4b|think is the only response that fails to name congee and largely omits the actual food content; it ranks last.

All models correctly report 'no visible text', which matches ground truth. No model flagged the image as possibly AI-generated, which the ground truth speculatively notes (this is acceptable since the AI-generation call is itself uncertain).

Image analyzer MCP returned 429 rate-limit during this evaluation; judgment was produced from the ground-truth block, the photo context, and the response texts, all of which are rich enough for confident scoring.
